channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: NSW coroner absolves police of blame for Pacific Motorway death
item.description: NSW Deputy State Coroner David O'Neil finds police acted within reason during a pursuit that led to a man being killed on the Pacific Motorway, north of Byron Bay.
item.dc:creator: Lachie McLeod

media:group.media:description: Narayan Rodwell died after a police chase in northern NSW in 2022.

item.title: Women's shed ex-board member banned over alleged transphobic comments
item.description: A former board member of a women's shed in Victoria has been evicted after allegedly "spewing" discriminatory and hurtful comments about transgender people.
item.dc:creator: Eden Hynninen

media:group.media:description: An ex-board member of the Ballarat Women's Shed has been accused of allegedly making anti-transgender comments.

item.title: US prosecutor denies launching criminal investigation into E Jean Carroll
item.description: Chicago's top federal prosecutor has denied his office has launched a criminal investigation into E Jean Carroll, who accused Donald Trump of raping her in the mid-1990s.

media:group.media:description: The federal prosecutor said a criminal investigation has never been opened into E Jean Carroll.

item.title: The 13-letter winning word in this year's Scripps National Spelling Bee
item.description: Correctly spelling 32 obscure words in just a minute and a half has won 14-year-old Shrey Parikh this year's Scripps National Spelling Bee.

media:group.media:description: Shrey Parikh's score of 32 words spelt correctly was a record for the lightning-round tiebreaker.

item.title: 'Gulf War III' pushes gold into bear market with no end in sight
item.description: Gold is now in a bear market after an enormous surge in the price of the precious metal to a record high earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: David Taylor

media:group.media:description: The price of gold has fallen 20 per cent from its record high of $US5,597 in late January.

item.title: Perth and WA's south set to be slammed by wild weather over long weekend
item.description: Perth and large parts of Western Australia's south are preparing for "an unusually strong and intense winter system" over the WA Day long weekend, with destructive wind gusts and heavy rainfall expected.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Mayes

media:group.media:description: Stormy weather is forecast to impact huge parts of southern Western Australia this weekend.

item.title: Violent teen on probation before causing fatal Melbourne crash
item.description: A Melbourne teenager who had been on probation for just nine days when he caused a deadly, high-speed crash is also facing court over a bashing that left a man with life-long disabilities.
item.dc:creator: Kristian Silva

media:group.media:description: The teenager was driving a stolen car when he crashed into another car and collided with a pole.

item.title: Kiwi goalkeeper gets bride's blessing to ditch wedding for World Cup
item.description: Auckland FC goalkeeper Michael Woud has had to put his wedding plans on hold after being selected in New Zealand's squad for the World Cup, but he says taking part in soccer's biggest tournament is not a bad reason to cancel.

media:group.media:description: Michael Woud has postponed his wedding after being a surprise addition to New Zealand's squad for the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Erin Patterson mushroom trial appeal set after 'miscarriage of justice' claim
item.description: Erin Patterson's appeal against her triple murder conviction will be heard in August, Victoria's Supreme Court has confirmed, with the director of public prosecutions also challenging the length of her jail term.

media:group.media:description: Erin Patterson was found guilty of the triple murder in July 2025.

item.title: James Valentine remembered as 'wonderful human being' at public memorial
item.description: The veteran broadcaster's family, friends and loyal listeners gathered in Sydney Town Hall to pay tribute to the 64-year-old who died last month, two years after he was diagnosed with cancer.

media:group.media:description: ABC Radio Sydney broadcaster James Valentine portrait for Australian Story, 2025.

item.title: Drag queen says Patagonia has launched trademark suit, claiming 'irreparable harm'
item.description: A drag queen and a major outdoor clothing brand have a fight over trademarks. Here's the feud explained.
item.dc:creator: Tessa Flemming

media:group.media:description: Pattie Gonia has urged outdoor apparel company Patagonia to drop its lawsuit.

item.title: Queensland prisoner loses appeal to freeze eggs
item.description: The Court of Appeal knocked back the woman's attempt to have her eggs frozen while she serves a ten year prison sentence.
item.dc:creator: Tara Cassidy

media:group.media:description: An incarcerated Queensland woman has lost her fight to have her eggs frozen while in custody.

item.title: Victorian MP sorry for mistakenly saying alleged koala killer had died
item.description: State MP Jacinta Ermacora says "they were misinformed and passed that misinformation on".
item.dc:creator: Jean Bell

media:group.media:description: The injured koala (not pictured) was subsequently euthanased as a result of its injuries.

item.title: Eels reveal Moses injury outlook ahead of Origin II
item.description: Parramatta do not have any concerns with the handling of Mitchell Moses's fitness in the NSW Blues set-up, as the Eels skipper faces a race against the clock to play before Game II of State of Origin.

media:group.media:description: Mitchell Moses missed the State of Origin opener with a soft tissue injury.

item.title: Investigators unable to find why plane engine failed due to fire damage
item.description: The pilot in his 60s suffered a spinal injury in the crash last year, as well as burns to 80 per cent of his body.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Hines

media:group.media:description: The aircraft crashed at Raglan and caught fire.

item.title: Coroner finds baby's death after home birth was preventable
item.description: A Victorian coroner has found a baby's death could have been avoided if his mother had been transferred to hospital hours earlier, after signs of an obstructed labour.
item.dc:creator: Anna McGuinness

media:group.media:description: 'Baby R' died at the Bendigo Hospital after his mother had an emergency caesarean.

item.title: Major winemaker considers moving business overseas if tax changes go through
item.description: The managing director of Taylors Wines in South Australia is considering leaving the country in response to the federal government's proposed tax changes.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kelly and Eugene Boisvert

media:group.media:description: Proposed tax changes could be the final straw for one major South Australian winemaker.

item.title: Man accused of ramming, shooting at car in Canberra's north faces court
item.description: The 20-year-old's defence lawyer said his client was needed at a barber shop, but Magistrate James Stewart said his circumstances did not meet the threshold for bail.
item.dc:creator: Zaarkacha Marlan

media:group.media:description: The man was arrested early this morning for his alleged actions on Monday night.

item.title: Health fears behind plea for road project redesign to be scrapped
item.description: After years spent modifying their Shoalhaven property to accommodate their son's special care needs, the Taylors fear noise, lights and disruption from a major road project could affect his health.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Fuller

media:group.media:description: Kim, Zeke, and Stephen Taylor at their home in Falls Creek.

item.title: Why younger generations are worse off than baby boomers
item.description: According to research from the Actuaries Institute, inequality between the younger and older generations is widening and could reach record levels within years.
item.dc:creator: Adelaide Miller

media:group.media:description: Mady and her family are finding it tough to balance the household budget.

item.title: Newly discovered ancient wasp species named after F1 driver Piastri
item.description: Biologists have named the wasp species Gwesped piastrii, honouring Australian Formula 1 driver Oscar Piastri.

media:group.media:description: It seems Australian Oscar Piastri has impressed more than just his F1 rivals and fans.

item.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes on launch pad during test
item.description: Billionaire Jeff Bezos's Blue Origin says it experienced an "anomaly", a term commonly used by rocket companies to describe a launch failure or explosion.

media:group.media:description: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket booster explodes during a state fire.

item.title: KPMG boss quits amid auditing scandal
item.description: KPMG Australia chief executive Andrew Yates has stepped down amid a scandal involving audit partners accessing confidential client documents.
item.dc:creator: Emily Stewart and Emilia Terzon

media:group.media:description: Andrew Yates says he takes accountability for the fallout.

item.title: Centralian Eisteddfod seeking funding support amid future uncertainty
item.description: The Centralian Eisteddfod Council has raised the alarm about funding challenges, which could mean the event will cease after next year.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Ellis

media:group.media:description: The Alice Springs Centralian Middle School Concert Band competed in the Centralian Eisteddfod and came first in their category.

item.title: Government defends blocking counterterror documents from Bondi royal commission
item.description: The federal government has made a public interest immunity claim over the documents before the Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion, essentially arguing it is not in the public interest for the documents to be released.
item.dc:creator: Tom Lowrey

media:group.media:description: Michelle Rowland says it is up to commissioner Virginia Bell to release the documents.
